description: Create Notebook 04: Credit Risk Model (for Banks). Binary classification model predicting UMKM survival/default probability. Includes AUC-ROC, calibration curves, score bands, and PD bucketing for regulatory compliance.
steps: SECTION 1 - Load & Prepare Data: Read data/umkm_engineered.csv. Define target: is_survived_3yr (1=survived, 0=defaulted). Select bank-relevant features: omset_bulanan, jumlah_karyawan, tahun_berdiri, has_digital_presence, jarak_ke_bank_terdekat, penetrasi_kur_pct, skor_infrastruktur, risiko_banjir, risiko_gempa, competition features, business_maturity.
SECTION 2 - Class Balance Analysis: Check class distribution. If imbalanced, note it and plan for SMOTE or class_weight balancing. Visualize class distribution.
SECTION 3 - Train/Test Split: Stratified 80/20 split by target variable. Verify both sets have similar class proportions.

SECTION 4 - Model Training: Train models: (a) Logistic Regression (interpretable baseline for banks), (b) XGBoost Classifier, (c) LightGBM Classifier, (d) Random Forest Classifier. Use class_weight='balanced' or scale_pos_weight for imbalance.

SECTION 5 - Model Evaluation: For each model compute: AUC-ROC curve, AUC-PR (Precision-Recall) curve, Classification report (precision, recall, F1), Confusion matrix. KS-Statistic (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) - important for banking: compute cumulative distributions of good and bad, find max separation.
SECTION 6 - Calibration: Plot calibration curves (reliability diagrams) for each model. Apply Platt scaling or isotonic regression if needed. Banks need well-calibrated probabilities.

SECTION 7 - Score Bands / Rating Mapping: Convert predicted probabilities to score bands (e.g., 300-850 like credit scores). Create rating buckets: AAA, AA, A, BBB, BB, B, CCC, CC, C. Map PD (probability of default) to each rating. Show migration matrix concept.

SECTION 8 - Regulatory Output (PD Bucketing): Group predicted PDs into regulatory buckets (0-1%, 1-3%, 3-5%, 5-10%, 10-20%, 20%+). For each bucket: count, actual default rate, average PD, expected loss. This is what banks submit to regulators (Basel III/IV style).

SECTION 9 - Feature Importance for Bank Decision: SHAP values focused on bank-interpretable features. Partial dependence plots for top 3 features. Decision boundary visualization. Generate 'reasons for decline' logic.
SECTION 10 - Save: Save best credit model to models/credit_risk_model.joblib. Save score bands table to data/credit_score_bands.csv. Save PD bucketing to data/pd_regulatory_buckets.csv.
Include extensive markdown explaining banking context, regulatory requirements, and model interpretation.
acceptance_criteria: Score bands table maps probability ranges to credit ratings
PD bucketing table has at least 5 buckets with count, actual default rate, and predicted PD
verification: Execute the notebook: jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute notebooks/04_Credit_Risk_Model.ipynb --ExecutePreprocessor.timeout=600 --output executed_04.ipynb --output-dir /tmp/
Check model: python -c "import joblib; model = joblib.load('models/credit_risk_model.joblib'); print(f'Model: {type(model).__name__}')"
Check outputs: python -c "import pandas as pd; bands = pd.read_csv('data/credit_score_bands.csv'); print(bands); assert len(bands) >= 5"
findings: Notebook executes successfully. Key finding: the is_survived_3yr target has very weak correlation with the features (max |r| < 0.07), so all models achieve AUC around 0.52. This is a characteristic of the synthetic data generation in Notebook 01. LightGBM selected as best model because tree-based models produce wider probability spread (0.07-0.98) vs. Logistic Regression (0.35-0.64), which is essential for meaningful PD buckets and score bands. Use n_jobs=1 for all models. sklearn PartialDependenceDisplay has bugs with binary features; used manual PDP computation instead. SHAP TreeExplainer works correctly with LGBMClassifier. Outputs: models/credit_risk_model.joblib (LGBMClassifier), data/credit_score_bands.csv (7 bands), data/pd_regulatory_buckets.csv (6 buckets). Use python3 for commands.
